description:
Update README to describe the workspace + the two-plugin model (forge and
issues), with a pointer to the SPEC-lifecycle-sync-participants rationale
for why they are separate participants. Rework Makefile so `make install`
installs both binaries to ~/.local/bin, `make check` runs the full gate
across the workspace, and `make build` builds both. Add a CONTRIBUTING-style
note documenting the disjoint-projection invariant.

WHY (philosophy in long-lived artifacts): future maintainers — including
agents in this repo — will reach for "just merge them into one binary, the
PAT's the same anyway" without the README explaining why the architecture
forbids it. Bake the why into the README, not the commit message that gets
lost in `git log`. The Makefile's `make check` is the contract; it's the
thing the pre-commit hook runs and the thing that future agents trust.

ACCEPTANCE:
- README explains: what the workspace is, what each binary does
  (one paragraph each), the disjoint-projection rule with one concrete
  example, and how to install/configure each plugin independently.
- README has explicit "don't merge these" note for future agents, citing
  SPEC-lifecycle-sync-participants §3 (projection) and §5 (Participant
  contract: one name).
- `make install` installs both bins (only balls-plugin-github exists at
  this point; entry for balls-plugin-github-issues stub-handled gracefully
  — `install -m 0755 ... || true` is wrong; instead Makefile lists only
  what exists today and Epic B's B7 adds the second).
- `make check` runs `cargo test --workspace`, workspace-wide clippy
  `-D warnings`, line-length check across all crates, coverage across the
  workspace at 100%.
title: README + Makefile rework: workspace install, both bins, multi-crate make check
